finding: After submitting the application the only confirmation is small green text beside the button; the form is not cleared and there is no redirect, even though a /thank-you-for-applying page exists.
persona_rationale: Every coaching/application funnel I have used on the competitor redirects to a dedicated confirmation page so the prospect knows the action landed; a whisper of green text made even the automation resubmit five times, and a real lead will do the same or assume it failed.

suggested_fix.current: Inline green text 'Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.' beside Submit, form stays filled, no redirect
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to /thank-you-for-applying on success (and clear the form), matching the existing dedicated confirmation page

finding: The Blog/News CMS has no entry in the studio sidebar; the entire content type is only reachable by typing /admin/news.

evidence.quote: navigation "Admin sections": link "SEO pages : SEO page content" ... link "Media library : Images and source assets"

persona_rationale: In WordPress, Posts is a top-level menu item; a CMS that hides an entire content type behind a URL reads as broken to an evaluator and would cost my team productivity every day.

suggested_fix.current: Sidebar 'Admin sections' = SEO pages, Media library only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Blog & News' link (→ /admin/news) to the studio sidebar 'Admin sections' nav

finding: The same content type is labelled four different ways: route /news, nav 'NEWS', public page H1 'BLOG', admin editor 'Blog CMS' / 'New blog post'.
persona_rationale: On the competitor one content type has one name everywhere; four names for one thing makes me distrust the rest of the information architecture and slows my team onboarding.

suggested_fix.current: nav 'NEWS' / H1 'BLOG' / editor 'Blog CMS' / route /news
suggested_fix.proposed: Pick one label (e.g. 'News') and use it across nav, page heading, route, and admin editor

finding: Contact is not in the header navigation; the only discoverable path is a footer 'CONTACT US' link.

evidence.quote: navigation "Primary": link "About" ... link "Resources" ... link "Case Studies" ... link "News"

persona_rationale: Competitor funnels keep Contact in the header or a sticky CTA; burying it in the footer makes me assume there is no way to reach a human before committing.

suggested_fix.current: Header Primary nav = About, Resources, Case Studies, News (no Contact)
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Contact' link to the header Primary nav

finding: The pages list has no row-level delete/duplicate/bulk actions; the '⋮' actions menu did not expose Archive/Delete and there is no multi-select.
persona_rationale: WordPress and Webflow let me delete, duplicate, and bulk-manage rows directly from the list; without it, routine content cleanup forces me to open every record, which is slower than my current tool.

suggested_fix.current: '⋮' row menu with no visible Delete/Duplicate; no bulk select
suggested_fix.proposed: Expose Edit / Duplicate / Archive / Delete in the row menu and add checkbox multi-select with bulk archive/delete

finding: The Redirects manager is an orphan route with zero inbound links from any explored admin page.
persona_rationale: Redirect management is a table-stakes SEO feature I rely on; if I can't find it from the UI I'll assume it doesn't exist and count that as a missing feature versus my current tool.

suggested_fix.current: /admin/pages/redirects reachable only by URL
suggested_fix.proposed: Link 'Redirects' from the SEO pages screen header and/or the studio sidebar

finding: On first open the editor's 'Quick Tour' overlay (Step 1 of 3) covers the very Builder outline panel it is pointing at and blocks interaction until dismissed.
persona_rationale: A power user switching tools wants to start building immediately; an overlay that obscures the panel it describes is the kind of forced tutorial friction that makes a polished editor feel clumsy.

suggested_fix.current: Quick Tour Step 1 overlay sits on top of the Builder outline panel it highlights
suggested_fix.proposed: Position the tour callout beside the panel (not over it) and add a persistent 'Skip tour' that is remembered per user

finding: A news listing thumbnail fails to load (500 on a Webflow CDN image).
persona_rationale: A broken image on the public blog is the first thing I'd screenshot in a switch-evaluation deck as evidence the product isn't production-solid.

suggested_fix.current: Article thumbnail points at a Webflow CDN URL returning 500
suggested_fix.proposed: Re-host the image in the Media library and reference the local asset; add a fallback placeholder for failed images

finding: Homepage has high layout shift (CLS 0.263) and horizontal scroll at mobile-375.

evidence.quote: Layout shift (CLS): 0.263 ... mobile-375 home-003-mobile.png | horizontal scroll present

persona_rationale: I judge a tool in the first 3 seconds; content jumping on load and sideways scroll on a phone signal a less mature front-end than the competitor I already use.

suggested_fix.current: CLS 0.263 on home; mobile horizontal scroll
suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve dimensions for hero media/fonts to bring CLS under 0.1 and clip the overflowing element at <=375px

finding: There is no global search across content types; search is scoped per-CMS ('Search SEO pages' only).

evidence.quote: textbox "Search SEO pages": /placeholder: Search title, keyword, or URL

persona_rationale: On the competitor one search box finds any piece of content; having to know whether something is an SEO page or a blog post before I can search for it is friction my team would feel constantly.

suggested_fix.current: Per-CMS search box ('Search SEO pages') with no cross-content search
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a studio-level global search that returns SEO pages, blog posts, and media in one result list

finding: /admin/settings has no settings home; it only redirects to /admin/settings/users, and the sidebar 'Settings' deep-links straight to Users.
persona_rationale: Competitor admins have a Settings landing page (general, integrations, billing, users); a Settings link that is really just Users implies the settings surface is thin.

suggested_fix.current: /admin/settings → redirect to /admin/settings/users; sidebar label 'Settings : Users and access'
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a Settings overview page listing all settings sections, or rename the sidebar item to 'Users & access'

finding: After saving a new SEO page the list verification raced and the page did not reliably appear; one run reported the created page 'not visible in /admin/pages list'.
persona_rationale: Save-then-confirm is the core CMS loop; if I save and the item isn't immediately and obviously in the list I lose trust that my work persisted, which is exactly what makes people abandon a new tool.

suggested_fix.current: New page does not reliably/immediately appear at top of list after save
suggested_fix.proposed: Revalidate the list on save and show a success toast with a direct link to the new page row

finding: The news list rows offer no delete/archive affordance; archiving requires opening each post's editor.

evidence.quote: no Delete control found in list row or editor

persona_rationale: Bulk-trashing posts from the list is something I do weekly in WordPress; per-post editor diving to retire content is a real productivity regression for me.

suggested_fix.current: No row-level delete/archive in /admin/news; editor-only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add row actions (Edit / Archive / Delete) and bulk select to the news list

finding: Content libraries (/admin/libraries) is reachable only via a 'Content libraries' link in the Media library header, not from the sidebar.
persona_rationale: Reusable content libraries are a feature I'd actively look for when switching; tucking them behind another page's header makes a real feature feel hidden.

suggested_fix.current: /admin/libraries linked only from the Media library header
suggested_fix.proposed: Surface 'Content libraries' as its own sidebar item under CONTENT

finding: The pre-call resources page contains 14 links and 0 embedded videos/iframes.
persona_rationale: Competitor pre-call pages embed the prep video inline so I don't bounce to YouTube and lose the thread; a wall of outbound links is a weaker prep experience.

suggested_fix.current: Pre-call page is a list of 14 links, no embedded media
suggested_fix.proposed: Embed the key prep video(s) inline on /pre-call-resources

finding: Readiness and status indicators in the pages list are 10x10px unlabeled buttons.

evidence.quote: small touch target: button "SEO readiness: Blocked" 10x10px ... button "Page status: Draft" 10x10px

persona_rationale: On the competitor status is a labelled pill I can read at a glance and click reliably; 10px dots force me to hover or guess and are unusable on a trackpad in a meeting.

suggested_fix.current: 10x10px colored-dot buttons for readiness/status
suggested_fix.proposed: Render readiness/status as labelled pills (e.g. 'Draft', 'SEO: Blocked') with >=24px hit area

finding: A 'Schedule failed' workflow filter is exposed in the pages list even though scheduling is otherwise not surfaced in the studio.
persona_rationale: An error-state filter visible before I've even seen the scheduling feature makes the studio feel like it's leaking internal states; my competitor only shows scheduling UI once I've scheduled something.

suggested_fix.current: 'Schedule failed' filter always visible in workflow filters
suggested_fix.proposed: Hide the 'Schedule failed' filter unless at least one page has a failed schedule

finding: Legacy /blog/* article paths 404 instead of redirecting to the /news/* equivalent.
persona_rationale: Migrating from a competitor, preserving old URLs is exactly the kind of thing I check; a 404 on the obvious /blog path tells me link equity and bookmarks will break on cutover.

suggested_fix.current: /blog/{slug} returns 404
suggested_fix.proposed: 301-redirect /blog/{slug} to /news/{slug} (use the existing redirects manager)

finding: Article cards are scroll-reveal animated and render as blank outlines above the fold before scrolling.
persona_rationale: First paint that looks empty makes me think the page is broken or still loading; the competitor renders cards immediately and animates subtly, so this reads as less finished.

suggested_fix.current: Cards start invisible until scrolled into view
suggested_fix.proposed: Render cards visible by default and apply a lightweight fade-in, or trigger the reveal on initial paint for above-the-fold cards

finding: Primary navigation links are short, ~17-20px tall hit targets (e.g. 'NEWS' 44x20px, 'HOME' 43x17px).
persona_rationale: Competitor nav targets are comfortably tappable on mobile; these thin links are easy to miss on a phone and would generate misclicks for my team.

suggested_fix.current: Header/footer nav links ~17-20px tall
suggested_fix.proposed: Increase nav link padding to a >=44px tap target on mobile

finding: The news editor's Save control was not findable on one corrected run ('FAILED: no visible Save control'), indicating an inconsistent/low-visibility save affordance.

evidence.quote: save draft (NOT publish) | FAILED: no visible Save control

persona_rationale: Save must be unmissable in any editor; if even careful navigation can't find it, I'll worry my draft is lost — the cardinal sin for a content tool.

suggested_fix.current: Save draft control intermittently not visible in news editor
suggested_fix.proposed: Pin a persistent 'Save draft' button in the editor header alongside Publish, always in view

finding: While building, the editor URL stays /admin/pages/new until the first save, so there is no shareable/bookmarkable page URL mid-edit.

evidence.quote: Editor URL stays at /admin/pages/new while building (no shareable page URL until saved)

persona_rationale: In Webflow I can hand a teammate a link to a draft I'm working on; not having a stable URL until save means I can't collaborate until I commit.

suggested_fix.current: URL stays /admin/pages/new until first save
suggested_fix.proposed: Create the draft record (and its URL) on entering the builder so the page is addressable immediately

finding: /admin/pages/block-preview-audit is an internal tool reachable only by URL with no inbound links.

evidence.quote: Block-preview-audit (/admin/pages/block-preview-audit) is likewise only reachable by URL

persona_rationale: Stray internal/debug routes shipping in the admin make me wonder what else is half-wired; the competitor keeps internal tooling out of the production studio.

suggested_fix.current: block-preview-audit internal route present in admin with no nav
suggested_fix.proposed: Gate internal audit tools behind a dev flag or remove them from the production admin build

finding: The state dropdown lists US states only, with no validation feedback shown on empty or invalid submit.

evidence.quote: empty: no visible validation errors after empty submit

persona_rationale: The brand and case studies read Australian (Adelaide), yet the apply form is US-states-only and gives no inline validation — if my prospects are international this form is a dead end and the silent validation compounds it.

suggested_fix.current: US-state-only dropdown; no inline validation on empty/invalid submit
suggested_fix.proposed: Support the actual target geography (or use a country + free-text region) and show inline per-field validation messages on submit
